Page.Width: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualWidth, FallbackValue=700}
Page.Height: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualHeight, FallbackValue=700}

Run.Text: As our airborne platforms search for radar contacts, you will begin to see radar echoes appear on the Tactical Display.

A radar echo just means that a surface contact has been detected at that position. No other information can be obtained from a radar echo alone. For our forces to classify enemy radar echoes, we need to correlate the radar echo with a hostile radar emission, a known hostile sound/acoustic signature, or (in rare cases) a visual ID (VID).
